channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: Manhattan Retail’s Recovery Is Real, But It’s a Tenant’s Market, Not a Landlord’s
item.description: David Yurman’s 22,000-SF Fifth Ave lease and REBNY’s Q1 report show Manhattan retail demand is real but narrow. Vacancies persist on Sixth Ave. Capital follows tenants, not landlords.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Retail’s Recovery Is Real, But It’s a Tenant’s Market, Not a Landlord’s
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan retail, REBNY, David Yurman, Fifth Avenue, retail leasing, tenant demand, storefront vacancies

item.title: Euro Capitulation Signals a Capital Cost Advantage That Reshapes CRE Allocation
item.description: Wall Street abandons euro strength bets as US rate hikes outpace Europe. For CRE capital markets, the signal is a widening cost-of-capital gap that favors dollar-based buyers and pressures European asset pricing.

news:news.news:title: Euro Capitulation Signals a Capital Cost Advantage That Reshapes CRE Allocation
news:news.news:keywords: euro, interest rates, capital flows, currency risk, CRE allocation

item.title: Dollar at 13-Month High: The CRE Capital Stack Just Got More Expensive
item.description: The dollar hit a 13-month high as foreign capital chases AI and Fed rate hikes. For CRE, this means tighter liquidity, higher hedging costs, and a narrower bid for US assets.

news:news.news:title: Dollar at 13-Month High: The CRE Capital Stack Just Got More Expensive
news:news.news:keywords: dollar, foreign investment, interest rates, liquidity, capital flows

item.title: New Rochelle's 60-Day Permit Clock Shows the Cost of Municipal Uncertainty
item.description: New Rochelle's Downtown Overlay Zone cut permitting to 60-90 days, attracting $1B+ in investment. The lesson for capital markets: time risk is the most expensive tax on development.

news:news.news:title: New Rochelle's 60-Day Permit Clock Shows the Cost of Municipal Uncertainty
news:news.news:keywords: New Rochelle, development, permitting, RXR Realty, municipal policy, capital flows, time risk

item.title: Coney Island III Tops Out: The Capital Stack Behind 420 Affordable Units
item.description: BFC Partners tops out 420-unit all-affordable building in Coney Island. Citi and Goldman Sachs provide construction debt and equity. The deal reveals how public subsidies enable private capital to work at scale.

news:news.news:title: Coney Island III Tops Out: The Capital Stack Behind 420 Affordable Units
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, BFC Partners, Citi, Goldman Sachs, NYC HPD, construction financing, public-private partnership

item.title: Washington's $50B Deferred Maintenance Is a Capital Stack Problem, Not a Budget Problem
item.description: GSA's $50B maintenance backlog reveals a capital allocation failure. Buildings deteriorate while Congress controls every dollar. The market should watch for asset sales, not repairs.

news:news.news:title: Washington's $50B Deferred Maintenance Is a Capital Stack Problem, Not a Budget Problem
news:news.news:keywords: GSA, deferred maintenance, federal buildings, capital allocation, public real estate

item.title: Manhattan Sublease Shrinkage Is a Landlord Power Move, Not Just AI Demand
item.description: Manhattan sublease space fell below 11M SF, half its 2022 peak. AI firms are leasing, but landlords are pulling space to chase higher direct rents. A capital markets signal.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Sublease Shrinkage Is a Landlord Power Move, Not Just AI Demand
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan office, sublease, AI leasing, landlord leverage, rent reset

item.title: Invel's €400M Fund Close Shows Southern Europe Is a Capital Destination, Not a Distress Play
item.description: Invel closes €400M oversubscribed opportunity fund for Greek and Italian real estate. The capital is betting on recovery, not distress.

news:news.news:title: Invel's €400M Fund Close Shows Southern Europe Is a Capital Destination, Not a Distress Play
news:news.news:keywords: Invel, Southern Europe, opportunity fund, Greece, Italy, institutional capital

item.title: Private Credit's CRE vs. Corporate Risk: Why Collateral Changes Everything
item.description: Prime Finance's John Atwater explains why commercial real estate credit and corporate direct lending carry different risks, and why CRE debt may now be the safer bet.

news:news.news:title: Private Credit's CRE vs. Corporate Risk: Why Collateral Changes Everything
news:news.news:keywords: private credit, commercial real estate debt, corporate direct lending, risk analysis, John Atwater

item.title: Madison Realty and Naftali Credit Back Brooklyn Condo: A Bet on Presales, Not Spec
item.description: Madison Realty Capital and Naftali Credit Partners provide $75M for a 131-unit condo project in Flatbush, Brooklyn. The deal signals private credit's selective appetite for for-sale development.

news:news.news:title: Madison Realty and Naftali Credit Back Brooklyn Condo: A Bet on Presales, Not Spec
news:news.news:keywords: Madison Realty Capital, Naftali Credit Partners, New Empire, condo financing, Brooklyn development

item.title: Sydney's Auction Weakness Signals a Capital Market Shift, Not Just a Housing Slowdown
item.description: Sydney's weakest auction clearance in six years signals more than a housing slowdown: it reveals tightening liquidity, higher cost of capital, and a repricing of risk that will reshape Australian CRE capital flows.

news:news.news:title: Sydney's Auction Weakness Signals a Capital Market Shift, Not Just a Housing Slowdown
news:news.news:keywords: Sydney, auction clearance, interest rates, property tax, capital markets, liquidity, repricing

item.title: Torkian's 30-Story Tower Shows Development Math Still Works Only With Air Rights and Subsidies
item.description: Torkian Group's 30-story, 70-unit tower at 301 East 71st Street relies on air rights, UAP density bonus, and all-electric design. The capital stack reveals what it takes to pencil new rental development in Manhattan.

news:news.news:title: Torkian's 30-Story Tower Shows Development Math Still Works Only With Air Rights and Subsidies
news:news.news:keywords: Torkian Group, Upper East Side, Manhattan development, air rights, UAP, rental housing, SLCE Architects

item.title: Alloy's 1,500-Unit Brooklyn Project Shows Development Is Back, but Only With Public Land and Political Capital
item.description: Alloy Development's 240 Nassau Street project in Vinegar Hill, Brooklyn, enters ULURP with 1,500 units, 300 affordable homes, a school, and community space. The deal shows large-scale rental development now depends on public land, political capital, and patient equity.

news:news.news:title: Alloy's 1,500-Unit Brooklyn Project Shows Development Is Back, but Only With Public Land and Political Capital
news:news.news:keywords: Alloy Development, ULURP, Vinegar Hill, Brooklyn, affordable housing, public-private partnership, development finance, NYC Educational Construction Fund

item.title: Permitting Reform Is Capital Policy: The Real Estate Cost Hidden in Regulatory Delay
item.description: Sen. Armstrong says permitting reform lowers housing and energy costs. For CRE capital markets, regulatory delay is a hidden tax on development returns and a constraint on new supply.

news:news.news:title: Permitting Reform Is Capital Policy: The Real Estate Cost Hidden in Regulatory Delay
news:news.news:keywords: permitting reform, housing costs, energy costs, regulatory delay, development returns, capital markets, supply constraint

item.title: The Dollar's 13-Month High Is a Capital Cost Signal for CRE
item.description: The dollar hit a 13-month high as foreign investors chase AI and Fed rate expectations. For CRE, this means more expensive foreign capital and tighter liquidity.

news:news.news:title: The Dollar's 13-Month High Is a Capital Cost Signal for CRE
news:news.news:keywords: dollar, foreign capital, interest rates, liquidity, capital costs

item.title: Warsh on Stage With 2008 Class Signals Central Banks Are Preparing for the Next Crisis
item.description: Kevin Warsh and three 2008 crisis veterans share a stage. The signal for CRE capital markets: central banks are preparing for private market stress, not just inflation.

news:news.news:title: Warsh on Stage With 2008 Class Signals Central Banks Are Preparing for the Next Crisis
news:news.news:keywords: Kevin Warsh, central banking, financial crisis, private credit, CRE risk, liquidity, regulation

item.title: ECB's Schnabel Warning: European CRE Faces a Longer Rate Squeeze
item.description: ECB's Schnabel warns of upside inflation risks despite peace deal, signaling delayed rate cuts and prolonged pressure on European CRE refinancing and valuations.

news:news.news:title: ECB's Schnabel Warning: European CRE Faces a Longer Rate Squeeze
news:news.news:keywords: ECB, inflation, interest rates, European CRE, refinancing risk

item.title: Mortgage Spreads, Not the Fed, Kept Housing Alive Through the Iran Shock
item.description: Housing demand held up during the Iran conflict because mortgage spreads compressed, not because the Fed blinked. The capital markets story behind the 2026 rate stability.

news:news.news:title: Mortgage Spreads, Not the Fed, Kept Housing Alive Through the Iran Shock
news:news.news:keywords: mortgage spreads, housing market, interest rates, capital markets, liquidity

item.title: BIS AI Bust Warning Is a Capital Markets Signal CRE Should Not Ignore
item.description: BIS warns AI bust threatens global growth and credit. For CRE capital markets, the signal is about liquidity, leverage, and the cost of debt in a downside scenario.

news:news.news:title: BIS AI Bust Warning Is a Capital Markets Signal CRE Should Not Ignore
news:news.news:keywords: BIS, AI, macro risk, credit markets, CRE capital

item.title: NYC Rent Freeze Rewrites the Underwriting Math for Stabilized Multifamily
item.description: NYC's rent freeze on stabilized units, with costs up 5.3%, compresses NOI, tightens lender underwriting, and accelerates distress for owners with floating-rate debt.

news:news.news:title: NYC Rent Freeze Rewrites the Underwriting Math for Stabilized Multifamily
news:news.news:keywords: rent stabilization, New York City, multifamily, policy risk, distress, lending

item.title: Damac's Surfside Zero-Sales Record Shows Price Is Not the Only Barrier to Entry
item.description: Damac Properties paid $120M for the Surfside collapse site, sold zero units in 18 months, and paused construction. The failure reveals that capital alone cannot buy market acceptance.

news:news.news:title: Damac's Surfside Zero-Sales Record Shows Price Is Not the Only Barrier to Entry
news:news.news:keywords: Damac, Surfside, condo development, luxury residential, market entry

item.title: Private Credit Real Estate Debt: The Income Trade That Sidesteps Ownership Risk
item.description: Institutional investors increasingly view private credit real estate debt as a stable income source with downside protection, as banks retreat and valuations remain uncertain.

news:news.news:title: Private Credit Real Estate Debt: The Income Trade That Sidesteps Ownership Risk
news:news.news:keywords: private credit, real estate debt, institutional investors, bank pullback, income strategy

item.title: AllianzGI's $744M APAC Fund Shows Private Credit Is Following Yield, Not Just Avoiding Risk
item.description: AllianzGI's $744M first close for an APAC private credit fund reveals institutional demand for secured CRE debt in higher-yield markets, not just risk avoidance.

news:news.news:title: AllianzGI's $744M APAC Fund Shows Private Credit Is Following Yield, Not Just Avoiding Risk
news:news.news:keywords: private credit, Asia Pacific, Allianz Global Investors, institutional capital, CRE debt

item.title: BofA's Three-Hike Forecast Misses the Real CRE Signal: Rate Cuts Are Dead
item.description: BofA forecasts three Fed rate hikes in 2026, but market pricing and a 4.51% 10-year yield argue otherwise. For CRE, the real signal is that rate cuts are off the table, keeping refinancing pressure on.

news:news.news:title: BofA's Three-Hike Forecast Misses the Real CRE Signal: Rate Cuts Are Dead
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, interest rates, monetary policy, CRE refinancing, Bank of America

item.title: Soloviev's $400 Rent Ask Tests Whether Manhattan Office Has a Ceiling or Just a Price
item.description: Stefan Soloviev targets $400 psf at 9 West 57th Street, a record that would test Manhattan office demand. The signal is about scarcity, not a broad market recovery.

news:news.news:title: Soloviev's $400 Rent Ask Tests Whether Manhattan Office Has a Ceiling or Just a Price
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan office, 9 West 57th Street, Soloviev Group, record rent, office leasing, top-of-market

item.title: A $1.34 Billion Arbitration Award Is a Warning About Joint Venture Capital Stacks
item.description: JAMS arbitrator awards Mohammad Honarkar $1.34B in fraud case tied to Laguna Beach portfolio. The ruling reveals how joint venture equity can become a source of risk, not liquidity.

news:news.news:title: A $1.34 Billion Arbitration Award Is a Warning About Joint Venture Capital Stacks
news:news.news:keywords: joint venture, fraud, capital stack, equity, liquidity, distress

item.title: Helaba's $112M Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Back, but Only for the Right Sponsor
item.description: Helaba lends $111.5M for a 264-unit apartment project in Plano, TX, at the former JCPenney HQ. The deal shows construction debt is available only for institutional sponsors with proven execution.

news:news.news:title: Helaba's $112M Loan Shows Construction Debt Is Back, but Only for the Right Sponsor
news:news.news:keywords: Helaba, construction financing, multifamily, Dallas, StreetLights Residential, Pritzker Realty Group, Plano, JCPenney

item.title: DigitalBridge's $1.1B ArcLight Deal Shows Power, Not Real Estate, Is Now the Data Center Bottleneck
item.description: DigitalBridge's $1.1B ArcLight acquisition and Alphabet's $4.75B Intersect deal show data center developers buying power developers to bypass grid delays. Capital is shifting from real estate to energy infrastructure.

news:news.news:title: DigitalBridge's $1.1B ArcLight Deal Shows Power, Not Real Estate, Is Now the Data Center Bottleneck
news:news.news:keywords: DigitalBridge, ArcLight, data centers, power acquisition, grid delays, hyperscalers, energy infrastructure, vertical integration, Alphabet, Intersect

item.title: CMBS Distress Rate Hits 11.86%: The Resolution Machine Is Not Keeping Pace
item.description: CMBS distress rate rose to 11.86% in May 2026. The 170 bps gap between special servicing and delinquency signals unresolved pressure ahead of 2026-2027 maturities.

news:news.news:title: CMBS Distress Rate Hits 11.86%: The Resolution Machine Is Not Keeping Pace
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, distress, special servicing, delinquency, office, multifamily, maturities, CRED iQ

item.title: Blackstone's $125M Refi Shows Private Credit Is Choosing Sponsors, Not Assets
item.description: Blackstone provides $124.6M refi for Harbor Group International's Alesio Urban Center in Irving, TX. The deal signals that private credit is flowing to sponsors with balance-sheet credibility and a defensible basis.

news:news.news:title: Blackstone's $125M Refi Shows Private Credit Is Choosing Sponsors, Not Assets
news:news.news:keywords: Blackstone, Harbor Group International, refinancing, private credit, multifamily

item.title: Tuscan Village Leasing Shows Mixed-Use Capital Is Betting on Experience, Not Just Rent
item.description: Four new tenants including Whole Foods and Free People sign at Tuscan Village, a 170-acre mixed-use development near Boston. The leasing reveals capital is flowing to projects with experiential retail and residential density.

news:news.news:title: Tuscan Village Leasing Shows Mixed-Use Capital Is Betting on Experience, Not Just Rent
news:news.news:keywords: mixed-use, retail leasing, Tuscan Village, Whole Foods, capital markets

item.title: Bryant Park Grill Eviction Shows the Real Estate Value of a Prime Lease
item.description: Bryant Park Grill's eviction after a $26M sales year shows how park operators are maximizing revenue from prime retail assets, a trend reshaping urban concessions.

news:news.news:title: Bryant Park Grill Eviction Shows the Real Estate Value of a Prime Lease
news:news.news:keywords: retail, lease, eviction, urban real estate, concessions

item.title: Affordable Housing Deliveries Are Shrinking, But Still Above Pre-2020 Levels. That Gap Is the Story.
item.description: Yardi Matrix shows affordable housing deliveries shrinking but remaining above pre-2020 levels. The capital market signal is about pipeline sustainability, not demand weakness.

news:news.news:title: Affordable Housing Deliveries Are Shrinking, But Still Above Pre-2020 Levels. That Gap Is the Story.
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, Yardi Matrix, multifamily supply, development pipeline, capital markets

item.title: Detroit's 'Build, Baby, Build' Summit Signals Industrial CRE's Capital Inflection
item.description: At the Reindustrialize Summit, policy and capital converge on defense-driven manufacturing. The signal for industrial CRE: capital is concentrating around assets tied to national security supply chains.

news:news.news:title: Detroit's 'Build, Baby, Build' Summit Signals Industrial CRE's Capital Inflection
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, manufacturing, defense, capital flows, policy

item.title: Heiberger and Lev's Flatiron Townhouse Shows Boutique Condo Capital Is Betting on Scarcity, Not Scale
item.description: Buttonwood and Wheelhouse plan a three-unit Flatiron townhouse with a $20M sellout. The deal signals development capital is chasing scarcity and wealthy buyer demand for turnkey luxury.

news:news.news:title: Heiberger and Lev's Flatiron Townhouse Shows Boutique Condo Capital Is Betting on Scarcity, Not Scale
news:news.news:keywords: boutique development, New York City condos, townhouse market, luxury housing, development capital

item.title: A $38M Power Center Refi Shows Retail Debt Is Available for the Right Sponsor and Basis
item.description: Gantry secures $38M permanent loan for Mesa Grand power center in Phoenix. Full-term IO, cash-out proceeds, and an institutional balance sheet lender reveal where retail debt is flowing.

news:news.news:title: A $38M Power Center Refi Shows Retail Debt Is Available for the Right Sponsor and Basis
news:news.news:keywords: retail refinancing, power center, Phoenix, Gantry, balance sheet lender, interest-only loan, cash-out refinance

item.title: Fed Governor's Quiet Period Dinner: A Capital Markets Signal on Rate Policy and CRE Risk
item.description: A Trump-appointed Fed official spoke at a Bank of America dinner during the Fed's quiet period. The event signals potential policy leaks and uneven market access.

news:news.news:title: Fed Governor's Quiet Period Dinner: A Capital Markets Signal on Rate Policy and CRE Risk
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, quiet period, monetary policy, Bank of America, CRE capital markets

item.title: The Graffiti Towers Sale Is Stalled Because the City Sees the Billboard, Not the Building
item.description: Kali Chaudhuri's bid for LA's Graffiti Towers faces delay as city questions financing. The real prize: a 50,000-sf LED billboard worth tens of millions annually.

news:news.news:title: The Graffiti Towers Sale Is Stalled Because the City Sees the Billboard, Not the Building
news:news.news:keywords: Oceanwide Plaza, Graffiti Towers, KPC Square, Kali Chaudhuri, distressed real estate, billboard revenue, Los Angeles, bankruptcy sale, capital structure

item.title: Fed's Inflation Gauge Signals CRE's Cost of Capital Is Not Coming Down
item.description: A hotter PCE inflation reading reinforces Fed rate-hike expectations, keeping CRE debt costs elevated and refinancing windows narrow for 2026 maturities.

news:news.news:title: Fed's Inflation Gauge Signals CRE's Cost of Capital Is Not Coming Down
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, inflation, interest rates, refinancing risk, cost of capital

item.title: The $87.5 Million Escrow Gap: How Title and Law Firms Became the Engine of CRE Fraud
item.description: A federal lawsuit alleges Riverside Abstract and Nussbaum Lowinger orchestrated $87.5M in escrow fraud through sham flips, inflated valuations, and concealed kickbacks. The case exposes a critical vulnerability in CRE capital flows.

news:news.news:title: The $87.5 Million Escrow Gap: How Title and Law Firms Became the Engine of CRE Fraud
news:news.news:keywords: fraud, escrow, title, law firm, valuation, leverage, 1031 exchange, Riverside Abstract, Nussbaum Lowinger

item.title: NYC's $1B Preservation Program: Capital Is Not the Constraint, Timing Is
item.description: NYC's $1B Supportive Preservation Program targets 30,000 units. The real signal is how public capital is stepping in where private debt cannot yet refinance.

news:news.news:title: NYC's $1B Preservation Program: Capital Is Not the Constraint, Timing Is
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, New York City, HPD, preservation, public capital, supportive housing

item.title: Aby Rosen's $81.5M Sale Shows the Price of Empty Trophy Assets
item.description: Aby Rosen sells 281 Park Ave South for $81.5M, a 40% discount from 2022 ask. The empty landmark shows how vacancy and carry costs reset pricing for even the most famous buildings.

news:news.news:title: Aby Rosen's $81.5M Sale Shows the Price of Empty Trophy Assets
news:news.news:keywords: Aby Rosen, RFR, 281 Park Avenue South, New York office, vacancy, basis reset, liquidity

item.title: Geopolitics Gave CRE Borrowers a 5-Basis-Point Gift. The Fed Just Took It Back.
item.description: US-Iran ceasefire drops 30-year mortgage rate to 6.47%, but Fed dot plot signals year-end rate of 3.8%. CRE borrowers face a narrow, volatile window for refinancing.

news:news.news:title: Geopolitics Gave CRE Borrowers a 5-Basis-Point Gift. The Fed Just Took It Back.
news:news.news:keywords: mortgage rates, Treasury yields, Federal Reserve, refinancing, commercial real estate

item.title: Affinius's $1B Data Center Fund Shows Institutional Capital Is Chasing Power, Not Real Estate
item.description: Affinius nears $1bn for its first data center fund, backed by La Caisse. The raise signals institutional capital is prioritizing power access and infrastructure over traditional real estate risk.

news:news.news:title: Affinius's $1B Data Center Fund Shows Institutional Capital Is Chasing Power, Not Real Estate
news:news.news:keywords: data centers, Affinius, La Caisse, institutional capital, infrastructure

item.title: Capital Group's $210M Buy Shows Office Ownership Is a Rent Decision, Not a Market Bet
item.description: Capital Group buys its 55-story DTLA HQ for $210M. The deal reveals that for creditworthy tenants, owning is now cheaper than leasing. A capital markets signal, not a market bet.

news:news.news:title: Capital Group's $210M Buy Shows Office Ownership Is a Rent Decision, Not a Market Bet
news:news.news:keywords: Capital Group, office, Los Angeles, owner-user, Brookfield, special servicing, vertical campus

item.title: Apollo CREF's Dissolution Shows Public REITs Can't Absorb Concentrated Losses
item.description: Apollo CREF dissolves after $82M write-off on 111 West 57th Street and other concentrated losses. The $9B portfolio sale to Athene reveals public REITs' inability to absorb illiquid credit risk.

news:news.news:title: Apollo CREF's Dissolution Shows Public REITs Can't Absorb Concentrated Losses
news:news.news:keywords: Apollo, CREF, mortgage REIT, liquidation, 111 West 57th Street, Athene, commercial real estate lending, credit losses

item.title: Beazer's $400M Refi Raises Dream Finders' Takeover Cost by $53 Million
item.description: Beazer Homes refinanced $400M in notes, adding a $53M make-whole penalty that raises Dream Finders' acquisition cost by ~$2 per share.

news:news.news:title: Beazer's $400M Refi Raises Dream Finders' Takeover Cost by $53 Million
news:news.news:keywords: Beazer Homes, Dream Finders Homes, M&A, refinancing, change of control

item.title: Brookfield's Ground-Lease JV: A Liquidity Trade, Not a Vote of Confidence
item.description: Brookfield takes a non-controlling stake in a Safehold ground-lease portfolio with a seven-year buyback option. The deal reveals the cost of public market capital and the liquidity trade beneath the headline.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield's Ground-Lease JV: A Liquidity Trade, Not a Vote of Confidence
news:news.news:keywords: Brookfield, Safehold, ground lease, joint venture, liquidity, public REIT, capital markets

item.title: Warsh's First Fed Meeting Confirms CRE's Higher-for-Longer Reality
item.description: Fed holds rates at 3.5%-3.75% as Warsh's first meeting signals no 2026 cuts. CRE faces $875B in maturing loans with refinancing pressure unchanged.

news:news.news:title: Warsh's First Fed Meeting Confirms CRE's Higher-for-Longer Reality
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, interest rates, Kevin Warsh, CRE refinancing, monetary policy

item.title: Yellowstone's $480M Loan Shows Conversion Capital Is Flowing to the Right Sponsor and Basis
item.description: Yellowstone secures $480M construction loan from Madison Realty Capital for 1740 Broadway office-to-residential conversion. The deal reveals how private credit is pricing conversion risk and sponsor credibility.

news:news.news:title: Yellowstone's $480M Loan Shows Conversion Capital Is Flowing to the Right Sponsor and Basis
news:news.news:keywords: Yellowstone Real Estate Investments, Madison Realty Capital, 1740 Broadway, office-to-residential conversion, construction financing
